semantic.role: MCP context priming state machine that gates tool invocation based on the current context mode (off, project-first, or search-first).
responsibilities: Define the MCPContextMode union type for context priming strategies.
Track whether project or search context has been primed via boolean flags.
Determine whether a tool call is allowed based on the current mode and the set of search-priming tools.
outOfScope: Actual tool execution or LLM interaction.
Low-level MCP protocol serialization or transport.
Project or search context data retrieval.
invariants: The module must remain a stable low-level infra component; callers should use it only through the intended MCP tool gating path, not reach into its internals.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Introduce a configurable gating mechanism for MCP context priming to control when tools that require project or search context can be invoked, improving resource efficiency and user experience.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Hardened credential store and optimized LLM summarization pipeline; no direct impact on this MCP context gate module.

publicSurface.description: Union type defining the three context priming modes: 'off', 'project-first', and 'search-first'. Used to configure MCP tool gating behavior.
Class that tracks priming state and provides methods to evaluate whether a given tool is allowed to execute based on the current mode. Consumed by MCP server and tool dispatch logic.

outOfScope: Actual tool execution or LLM interaction.
